hypothesis: Combining OBV trend confirmation with a stochastic oversold/overbought reversal and a volatility filter ensures entries only when volume momentum, momentum rebound, and sufficient market movement align, reducing whipsaws in the highly liquid SOLUSDC market.

A rising OBV confirms a bullish trend, a stochastic above 80 signals momentum strength, and a high ATR ensures sufficient volatility; together they filter out weak or choppy markets, improving entry quality and risk-adjusted returns.

Combining OBV trend confirmation with a momentum check on the stochastic oscillator at extreme levels limits entries to moments when price and volume are both pushing in the same direction, while the ATR filter ensures only trades in a sufficiently volatile regime are taken, reducing whipsaws and improving risk‑to‑reward.

Combining OBV trend confirmation, stochastic momentum extremes, and rising ATR volatility filters out weak moves; only when all three align does a trade enter, and reversal of any two signals triggers exit, limiting exposure to false breakouts.
